shortdesc.ph: Used to configure parameters for screen sharing.

pd.note: When setting encoding resolution in document sharing scenarios, refer to the recommended settings.
pd.ph: The encoding resolution of the screen sharing video stream. The default value is 1920 × 1080, which is 2,073,600 pixels. Agora calculates the cost based on this parameter. If the screen size differs from this parameter, the SDK adopts the following encoding strategy. Assuming dimensions is set to 1920 × 1080:

ul.li: If the screen size is smaller than dimensions, for example, 1000 × 1000 pixels, the SDK uses the screen size (i.e., 1000 × 1000 pixels) for encoding.

If the screen size is larger than dimensions, for example, 2000 × 1500 pixels, the SDK encodes using the maximum value not exceeding dimensions while maintaining the screen aspect ratio (4:3), which is 1440 × 1080.

plentry.pd: On Windows and macOS, it represents the encoding frame rate (fps) of the screen sharing video stream. The default value is 5. Agora does not recommend setting a value higher than 15.

On Windows and macOS, it represents the encoding bitrate (Kbps) of the screen sharing video stream. The default value is 0, meaning the SDK automatically calculates the bitrate based on the current screen resolution.

pd.note: Due to macOS system limitations, setting to is invalid during screen sharing (not affected when sharing a window).
pd.ph: Whether to capture the mouse during screen sharing:

pd.note: Due to macOS system limitations, when setting this parameter to bring the window to the foreground, if the current application has multiple windows, only the main window will be brought to the foreground.
pd.ph: When calling the method to share a window, whether to bring the window to the foreground:

ul.li: : Bring the window to the foreground.
: (default) Do not bring the window to the foreground.

plentry.pd: List of window IDs to be excluded. When calling to start screen sharing, use this parameter to exclude specified windows. When calling to update screen sharing configuration, use this parameter to dynamically exclude specified windows.
The number of windows to be excluded. On the Windows platform, the maximum value of this parameter is 24; if it exceeds this value, window exclusion will fail.
(Applicable only to macOS and Windows) The width of the border (pixels). The default value is 5, and the value range is (0, 50]. This parameter takes effect only when highLighted is set to .

(Applicable only to macOS and Windows)
On the Windows platform, the border color is in ARGB format. The default value is 0xFF8CBF26.
On macOS, COLOR_CLASS represents NSColor.

pd.note: When sharing part of a window or screen, if this parameter is set to , the SDK will display a border around the entire window or screen.
pd.ph: (Applicable only to macOS and Windows) Whether to display a border around the shared window or screen:

pd.note: Due to system limitations, only Android API level 29 and above (i.e., Android 10 and above) support capturing system audio.
pd.ph: Whether to capture system audio during screen sharing:

plentry.pd: Audio configuration parameters. See for details. This parameter takes effect only when captureAudio is .
